LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GORDON GETTY, ROBERT BARNES, GILBERT MANAGEMENT, INC. AND VALLEJO INVESTMENTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE DEMURRER AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS, IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND OR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND THAT ESTABLISH THAT THIS CLAIM IS NOT TIME BARRED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, UPON SEEING THE NAME "ROBERT LEBERMAN" IN THE DOCUMENT, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE KNOWS ROBERT LEBERMAN, THAT SHE SEES HIM SOCIALLY ONCE A YEAR AT A MUTUAL FRIEND'S HOME, AND THAT THIS WILL NOT AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS GORDON GETTY'S, ROBERT BARNES', GILBERT MANAGEMENT, INC.'S, AND VALLEJO INVESTMENTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND (10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER) TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH ELEMENTS OF INTERNAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Gilda Turitz, Esq. of Sideman & Bancroft LLP, Tel. 415.733.3920, for defendants and moving party;
Kevin McBride, Tel. 415.800.3001, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: Plaintiff did not give notice that he will be appearing to contest the tentative ruling. The Court did not hear arguments and adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GORDON GETTY, ROBERT BARNES, GILBERT MANAGEMENT, INC. AND VALLEJO INVESTMENTS, INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. No Opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-31-2014 Continued To Apr-02-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Continued To Feb-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KEVIN MCBRIDE, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ELLEN LIU, ESQ., 415.392.1960, FOR DEFENDANTS GORDON GETTY, ROBERT BARNES, GILBERT MANAGEMENT, INC. AND VALLEJO INVESTMENTS, INC.

Judge Miller was shown for the first time at the hearing a copy of a document filed by Plaintiff entitled "Plaintiff's Statement re Order Sustaining Defendants Gordon Getty, Robert Barnes, Gilbert Management, Inc., Vallejo Investments, Inc. and Does I through X's Demurrer to Plaintiff' Complaint," which plaintiff indicated he had filed on November 12, 2013. Upon seeing the name "Robert Leberman" in the document, Judge Miller disclosed that she knows Robert Leberman, that she sees him socially once a year at a mutual friend's home, and that this will not affect her ability to be fair and impartial. Judge Miller asked Mr. McBride and counsel for defendants whether they had any questions about her disclosure. They did not.

RULING: Argued and the Court withdrew its tentative ruling. Defendants GORDON GETTY'S, ROBERT BARNES', GILBERT MANAGEMENT, INC.'S, and VALLEJO INVESTMENTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with leave to amend (10 days from notice of entry of order) to allege in good faith elements of internal infliction of emotional distress. Counsel for defendants to prepare a form of order (approved as to form and content). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KEVIN MCBRIDE, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ELLEN LIU, ESQ., 415.392.1960, FOR DEFENDANTS GORDON GETTY, ROBERT BARNES, GILBERT MANAGEMENT, INC. AND VALLEJO INVESTMENTS, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANTs GORDON GETTY, ROBERT BARNES, GILBERT MANAGEMENT, INC. and VALLEJO INVESTMENTS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Granted. No opposition filed. The demurrer as to the first cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend to allege facts, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, sufficient to state a cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and or negligent infliction of emotional distress, and that establish that this claim is not time barred. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
